steps.title: Check current clickhouse setup
steps.description:
Verify your clickhouse version and configuration:
```bash
clickhouse-client --version
```
This feature requires a working clickhouse installation.

steps.title: Review clickhouse configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant clickhouse configuration:
We already have "data skipping" indices in ClickHouse.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Inverted Indices (RFC)
steps.description:
Resoves #19970

A (generalized) inverted index for ClickHouse developed by  and 

This inverted index is implemented as a new type of skipping index. The implementation is well-aligned with ClickHouse's secondary/skipping index architecture, including index creation/alter/drop syntax, block stream
```yaml
`gin()` (or `gin(0)`) set the tokenizer to "tokens", whereas `gin(n)` with `n` between `2` and `8` sets "ngrams(n)” as tokenizer.

Main implementation techniques include, Roaring Bitmap(for postings lists) and FST(for term dictionaries), see the following references:
1. Single-pass index construction with segmentation, see Heinz, Steffen, and Justin Zobel. 2003. [Efficient single-pass index construction for text databases](https://doi.org/10.1002/asi.10268). JASIST 54(8):713–729.
2. Roaring Bitmaps, see https://github.com/RoaringBitmap/RoaringBitmap
3. Finite State Transducer (FST) Minimizatio
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Inverted Indices (RFC)" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Resoves #19970

A (generalized) inverted index for ClickHouse developed by  and 

This inverted index is implemented as a new type of skipping index. The implementation is well-aligned with ClickHouse's secondary/skipping index architecture, including index creation/alter/drop syntax, block stream piping, expression (RPN) evaluation, etc.
codeSnippets:
`gin()` (or `gin(0)`) set the tokenizer to "tokens", whereas `gin(n)` with `n` between `2` and `8` sets "ngrams(n)” as tokenizer.

Main implementation techniques include, Roaring Bitmap(for postings lists) and FST(for term dictionaries), see the following references:
1. Single-pass index construction with segmentation, see Heinz, Steffen, and Justin Zobel. 2003. [Efficient single-pass index construction for text databases](https://doi.org/10.1002/asi.10268). JASIST 54(8):713–729.
2. Roaring Bitmaps, see https://github.com/RoaringBitmap/RoaringBitmap
3. Finite State Transducer (FST) Minimization, see Mihov, S., Maurel, D. (2001). [Direct Construction of Minimal Acyclic Subsequential Transducers](https://doi.org/10.1007/3-540-44674-5_18). In: Yu, S., Păun, A. (eds) Implementation and Applica

prerequisites.description: A working clickhouse installation or development environment.
